no-restricted-syntax.message: no-silent-catch: catch body is empty. Per CLAUDE.md §1, every catch must re-throw, surface to the user (toast on client), or log via logger/Sentry breadcrumb on server. If intentional (best-effort localStorage etc.), add a one-line // ok: reason comment and a minimal logger.debug/warn.
no-silent-catch: catch body contains only console.log. Use logger.warn/error (or toast on client) so errors surface in production — console.log is dropped above info level in Vercel prod.

no-bare-nextresponse-json: API responses must use the canonical envelope per CLAUDE.md §2 — `{ success: true, data: T }` on success, `{ success: false, error: string }` on failure. Bare shapes have caused 3+ 'save doesn't work' bugs. If the object is built dynamically, assign it to a typed variable and pass that instead of an inline literal.

no-silent-catch: catch body is empty. Per CLAUDE.md §1, every catch must re-throw, surface to the user (toast on client), or log via logger/Sentry breadcrumb on server. If intentional (best-effort localStorage etc.), add a one-line // ok: reason comment and a minimal logger.debug/warn.
no-silent-catch: catch body contains only console.log. Use logger.warn/error (or toast on client) so errors surface in production — console.log is dropped above info level in Vercel prod.
